0-1-2-5-8_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-5-8_b0.note: Fm6b13/C is correct; structural fit beats Dm7(b5)/C (Db avoids -3 M7 penalty as b13 alteration); oracle split (music21->Dm7b5/C, tonal->DbM7b9/C) confirms genuine 3-way ambiguity; sound

0-1-2-4-6-10_b10.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-4-6-10_b10.note: F♯7(♯11,♭13)/A♯ is preferred because it is a complete tritone-related dominant with its major third in the bass, while C9♭5♭9/B♭ is a complete altered dominant in third inversion with simultaneous flat and natural ninths. Tonal supports the F♯ reading and music21 supports the C reading, confirming genuine ambiguity. Related bass cases consistently favor whichever dominant receives the more conventional bass role.

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b2.note: F♯7(♯9,♯11,♭13)/D is preferred because the voicing contains the complete F♯ altered-dominant shell F♯-A♯-C♯-E with D as flat thirteenth and A/G𝄪 as sharp-nine color. Tonal agrees as F#7#9#11b13/D, and ChoCo common-name counts favor dominant7 sharp-nine sharp-eleven flat-thirteen signatures over augmented-major-nine variants. Music21's B♭aug(maj9)/D add D♭,E is a related add-tone spelling, but it misses the complete dominant organization.

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b4.note: F♯7(♯9,♯11,♭13)/E is preferred because E is the flat seventh of a complete F♯ altered dominant with sharp-nine, sharp-eleven, and flat-thirteen color. Tonal agrees. Music21's B♭aug(maj9)/E add C♯ preserves an augmented-major upper structure, but it demotes the dominant seventh and altered-dominant function to added tones.

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b9.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b9.note: F♯7(♯9,♯11,♭13)/G𝄪 is preferred because A/G𝄪 is the sharp ninth of the complete F♯ altered-dominant stack. Tonal agrees semantically as F#7#9#11b13/A. Music21's B♭aug(maj9)/A add C♯,E names a related augmented-major shell, but it is less idiomatic than the complete altered-dominant reading and ChoCo strongly favors the dominant signature.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b0.note: F♯9(♯11,♭13)/B♯ is preferred because the voicing contains the complete F♯ dominant stack F♯-A♯-C♯-E plus G♯, B♯, and D. Tonal agrees with F#9#11b13/C. Music21's B♭9♯5/C add E,D♭ is a related altered-fifth bookkeeping label, but it omits the direct Lydian-dominant-flat-thirteen identity.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b1.note: F♯9(♯11,♭13)/C♯ is preferred because C♯ is the fifth of the complete F♯9♯11♭13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's C♯m(maj7) add F♯,D,A♯ re-roots the same pitch material away from the complete dominant shell.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b2.note: F♯9(♯11,♭13)/D is preferred because D is the flat thirteenth of a complete F♯ Lydian-dominant-flat-thirteen sonority. Tonal agrees. Music21's C9♯5/D add C♯,F♯ preserves some altered-dominant color but misses the complete F♯ dominant organization.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b4.note: F♯9(♯11,♭13)/E is preferred because E is the dominant seventh of the complete F♯9♯11♭13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's E9♯5 add C♯,A♯ is a related altered-fifth add-tone spelling, but it treats F♯ as the ninth of E rather than the clearer dominant root.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b6.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b6.note: F♯9(♯11,♭13) is preferred because it is root position and names every tone as part of a complete dominant stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's F♯9 add B♯,D keeps the root but drops the sharp-eleventh and flat-thirteenth identity into add tones.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b8.note: F♯9(♯11,♭13)/G♯ is preferred because G♯ is the ninth of the complete F♯9♯11♭13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's G♯7♭5 add E,A♯,C♯ describes related pitch classes but loses the complete F♯ dominant shell.

0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-2-4-6-8-10_b10.note: F♯9(♯11,♭13)/A♯ is preferred because A♯ is the major third of the complete F♯9♯11♭13 stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's A♭9♯5/B♭ add D,D♭ is an enharmonic altered-fifth bookkeeping label rather than the direct Lydian-dominant-flat-thirteen reading.

0-1-2-5-8_b5.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-5-8_b5.note: Fm6b13 is correct; F-Ab-C-D is a complete root-position Fm6 (enharmonically Dm7b5/F), while Db is a dissonant b13/lower-neighbor color; Dbmaj7b9/F is equally complete but inverted and requires Ebb for its b9; oracle split confirms genuine ambiguity; no engine change needed

0-1-3-5-8-9_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-8-9_b8.note: F7(♯9,♭13)/G♯ is preferred because F, A, C, and E♭ form a complete dominant seventh, with G♯/A♭ as sharp-nine bass color and D♭ as flat thirteenth. Tonal agrees as F7#9b13/Ab. Music21's A augmented-major-seventh add-tone spelling names a plausible upper structure but re-roots away from the complete altered-dominant shell that musicians would usually treat as primary.

0-1-3-5-7_b5.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7_b5.note: C♯maj9♭5/E♯ is preferred because C♯-E♯-G-B♯-D♯ forms a complete major-seventh-flat-five ninth sonority, with E♯ as the major third in the bass and every sounding pitch named directly. Tonal agrees as D♭M9♭5/F. Music21's Cm/F add D♭ keeps a C-minor shell but treats F and D♭ as additions, missing the complete C♯/D♭ major-seventh-flat-five organization.

0-1-3-5-7_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7_b7.note: C♯maj9♭5/G is preferred because G is the flat fifth of the complete C♯maj9♭5 structure, while C, E♯/F, and D♯/E♭ supply the major seventh, third, and ninth. Tonal agrees as D♭M9♭5/G. Music21's Cm/G add F,D♭ describes a related C-minor shell but demotes the major-third role and is less complete as a musician-facing chord symbol.

0-1-3-6-9_b9.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-6-9_b9.note: D#m7b5(add13)/A is defensible; the set is the symmetric dim7 {C,Eb,Gb,A} plus the chromatic Db, so root assignment is inherently ambiguous and every source picks a different root (whatchord D#, music21 F#, tonal Db). It is not a functional dominant: the notes are a subset of the C half-whole octatonic but lack any 3rd and b7, so no tritone. Our #1 is a complete m7b5 with a natural 13 and the bass (b5) as a core tone, and wins a 0.10 near-tie over Cdim7(b9) via natural-extension-over-altered-color. music21's F#o7/AaddC# is enharmonically identical to our F#m6#11/A reading (same root, intervals {0,3,6,7,9}, bass A), but we rank that #3 at delta 0.22, below the near-tie cutoff, so it does not surface; among the readings the app actually shows (#1 and the #2 Cdim7(b9) near-tie) no oracle agrees. tonal roots on Db, matching nothing of ours. The intuitive bass-rooted Adim7+chromatic description ranks low because Db will not sit cleanly on Adim7. Genuine ambiguity; no engine change warranted.

0-1-3-8_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-3-8_b1.note: D♭maj7sus2 is correct, and stronger than the b0 sibling: with D♭ in the bass it is a complete root-position reading (all 4 notes as core template tones, bass = root, every interval consonant), so it wins on structural fit beyond the tie-break window. Ab/Db (music21, pychord) roots on the complete Ab triad but strands D♭ as a natural 11, the avoid-note minor 9th against the major third (C); that C/D♭ minor-9th pair is better read as root+maj7 (our D♭) than as two non-root color tones. tonal also re-roots away from Ab (Cm(b6,b9)/Db), so the split is triad-first matchers vs harmonic reasoners, not a true Ab consensus; ours and tonal agree on rejecting Ab as root. Genuine ambiguity; sound; no engine change needed.

0-1-3-4-7-9_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-7-9_b0.note: A7(♯9,♯11)/B♯ is preferred because A, C♯, E, and G form a complete dominant seventh and C/E♭ supply conventional altered-dominant ♯9/♯11 color. Tonal agrees with the A7♯9♯11 reading. Music21's Am7/C add E♭,C♯ keeps C as a minor third and treats the major third C♯ as an added chromatic tone, which is valid pitch-class bookkeeping but less idiomatic for chord-symbol analysis of this split-third altered dominant.

0-1-3-4-7-9_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-7-9_b4.note: A7(♯9,♯11)/E is preferred because A, C♯, E, and G form a complete dominant seventh with the fifth in the bass, while C and E♭ are conventional ♯9/♯11 altered-dominant colors. Tonal agrees. Music21's Am7/E add E♭,C♯ treats the dominant third as an added chromatic tone rather than the chord-defining third, so it is less idiomatic for WhatChord's musician-facing symbol.

0-1-3-4-7-9_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-7-9_b7.note: A7(♯9,♯11)/G is preferred because A, C♯, E, and G form a complete third-inversion dominant seventh, with C and E♭ reading as standard ♯9/♯11 altered colors. Tonal agrees. Music21's Am7/G add E♭,C♯ is a valid alternate spelling of the same pitch classes, but it demotes the dominant third to an added tone and is less useful as the primary chord symbol.

0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b0.note: A13(♯9,♯11)/B♯ is preferred because A, C♯, E, G, and F♯ form a complete dominant thirteenth with conventional ♯9/♯11 altered color. Tonal agrees with A13♯9♯11. Music21's F♯øb9/C add E♭,C♯ is valid pitch-class bookkeeping, but it re-roots away from the complete altered dominant and is less idiomatic as the primary chord symbol.

0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b1.note: A13(♯9,♯11)/C♯ is preferred because the bass is the dominant third and the voicing contains the complete A13 altered-dominant stack A-C♯-E-G-F♯ plus ♯9/♯11 color. Tonal agrees. Music21's F♯m7/C♯ add E♭,G,C describes related pitch material but demotes the dominant function.

0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b3.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b3.note: A13(♯9,♯11)/D♯ is preferred because D♯ is the ♯11 of a complete A13 altered dominant. Tonal agrees. Music21's A diminished-seventh add-tone spelling keeps the A root but treats the major third, fifth, and thirteenth as chromatic additions, making it less useful as a musician-facing chord symbol.

0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b4.note: A13(♯9,♯11)/E is preferred because the bass is the dominant fifth and the voicing contains root, third, fifth, seventh, thirteenth, ♯9, and ♯11. Tonal agrees. Music21's F♯øb9/E add E♭,C♯ re-roots the same pitch classes into a half-diminished add-tone label rather than the complete altered dominant.

0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b7.note: A13(♯9,♯11)/G is preferred because the bass is the dominant seventh and the voicing contains the complete A13 altered-dominant stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's F♯øb9/G add E♭,C♯ is a valid alternate pitch-class organization but less idiomatic as the primary symbol.

0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b9.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-6-7-9_b9.note: A13(♯9,♯11) is preferred because it is root position and contains the complete altered dominant thirteenth: A, C♯, E, G, F♯, B♯, and D♯. Tonal agrees. Music21's F♯øb9/A add C♯,E♭ prioritizes a half-diminished structure over the clearer root-position dominant.

0-1-3-4-7-10_b3.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-4-7-10_b3.note: E♭13♭9 is preferred because E♭, G, B♭, and D♭ form a complete root-position dominant seventh, with C as the thirteenth and F♭ as flat-nine color. Tonal agrees with E♭13♭9. Music21's Cm7/E♭ add D♭,F♭ preserves a C-minor shell but demotes the root-position dominant structure to added chromatic tones; ChoCo common-name counts strongly favor dominant-seventh and flat-nine families over minor7 flat-nine/add-tone variants.

0-1-3-5-7-10_b3.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7-10_b3.note: E♭13 is preferred because E♭, G, B♭, and D♭ form a complete root-position dominant seventh with F and C as the upper extension stack. Tonal agrees with E♭13. Music21's Cm7/E♭ add F,D♭ is an upper-structure minor-seventh bookkeeping label; ChoCo common-name counts include thousands of dominant-thirteenth-stack observations but only sparse minor7 add-eleven variants.

0-1-3-5-7-9_b3.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7-9_b3.note: E♭13♯11 is preferred because E♭, G, D♭, F, A, and C form a root-position Lydian-dominant thirteenth stack. Tonal agrees on the E♭ dominant root but normalizes the tritone as flat five rather than sharp eleven; WhatChord's sharp-eleven spelling is clearer with the ninth and thirteenth present. Music21's D♭aug(maj9)/E♭ add G is an upper-structure add-tone spelling that misses the complete dominant organization.

0-1-3-5-7-10_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7-10_b7.note: E♭13/G is preferred because the bass is the dominant third and the voicing contains the complete E♭ dominant thirteenth stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's Cm7/G add D♭,F re-roots the same pitch material into a C-minor shell and treats the dominant seventh and ninth as additions, which is less idiomatic for this complete dominant inversion.

0-1-3-5-7-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-5-7-10_b10.note: E♭13/B♭ is preferred because the bass is the dominant fifth and the voicing contains the complete E♭ dominant thirteenth stack. Tonal agrees. Music21's Cm7/B♭ add F,D♭ describes related upper-structure material but misses the more direct dominant-root organization.

0-1-3-6-7-10_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-6-7-10_b7.note: E♭13♯9/G is preferred because the bass is the dominant third and the voicing contains E♭, G, B♭, D♭, C, and G♭/F♯ as a complete sharp-nine dominant thirteenth. Tonal agrees with E♭13♯9/G. Music21's Cm7/G add D♭,G♭ keeps a C-minor shell and treats the dominant colors as additions, which is less useful as the primary chord symbol.

0-1-4-6-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-10_b0.note: C7b5b9 is correct; it is a complete root-position altered dominant, while F#7#11/C is a complete tritone-related dominant with the #11 in the bass; both are conventional and similarly common in ChoCo, but the C bass favors the current root-position reading; key context respells the alternate without changing the ranking; no engine change needed

0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-2-4-6-9-10_b0.note: C13(♭9,♯11) is preferred because C is the bass/root and the voicing contains the complete C dominant shell C-E-B♭ with D♭, D, F♯, and A as altered and extended dominant color. Tonal's F♯7♯9♯11♭13/C is also a complete tritone-related dominant, and ChoCo has comparable support for the broad C Lydian-dominant-thirteenth family and the F♯ sharp-nine sharp-eleven flat-thirteen family. The C bass makes the root-position label defensible, but this is a genuine tritone-dominant ambiguity.

0-1-4-6-10_b4.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-10_b4.note: C7♭5♭9/E is preferred because it is a complete altered dominant with its major third in the bass, while F♯7♯11/E is the complete tritone-related dominant in third inversion. Music21 and tonal choose the valid F♯ reading, but the two chord signatures are similarly common in ChoCo and the E bass gives the C-rooted interpretation the more conventional inversion. Related bass cases consistently favor whichever dominant receives the stronger bass role.

0-1-4-6-7-10_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-7-10_b1.note: F♯7(♭9,♯11)/C♯ is preferred because C♯ is the fifth of a complete F♯ altered dominant, while C7♭9♯11/D♭ is the tritone-related dominant with its flat ninth in the bass. Music21 supports the F♯ root while tonal lists both and ranks the C reading first, so this is a genuine tritone-dominant ambiguity; the stable fifth bass favors WhatChord's F♯ label.

0-1-4-6-7-10_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-6-7-10_b7.note: C7(♭9,♯11)/G is preferred because G is the fifth of a complete C altered dominant and tonal agrees. Music21's C7/G add D♭,G♭ keeps the C dominant shell but treats the flat ninth and sharp eleventh as add tones, so the split reflects oracle vocabulary rather than an engine issue.

0-1-4-6-7-10_b10.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-7-10_b10.note: F♯7(♭9,♯11)/A♯ is preferred because A♯/B♭ is the major third of the complete F♯ altered dominant, while C7♭9♯11/B♭ is the tritone-related dominant in third inversion. Tonal lists both and ranks C first; music21 supports the F♯/G♭ root. The bass role makes the current F♯ label defensible in this context-free analysis.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b0.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b0.note: C7#5(b9,#11) is preferred because it names the complete C altered dominant C-E-Bb plus Db, F#, and G#/Ab. Tonal agrees and also lists the valid tritone-related F#9#11/C alternate; music21 prefers that F# reading. The split is a real altered-dominant vs tritone-sub ambiguity, but the C bass and complete altered stack justify WhatChord's primary label.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b1.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/Db is preferred because the pitch set is the complete C altered dominant with Db as flat-nine in the bass. Tonal agrees and also lists F#9#11/Db; music21 prefers the same valid tritone-related F# structure. This is a genuine dominant-function ambiguity, with WhatChord choosing the direct altered-dominant spelling.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b4.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b4.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/E is preferred because E is the major third of the complete C altered dominant, while F#9#11/E is the valid tritone-related dominant with its flat seventh in the bass. Tonal agrees with the C reading and lists F#9#11/E as the alternate; music21 prefers the F# label, confirming a genuine split rather than a clear oracle winner.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b8.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b8.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/G# is preferred because G#/Ab is the sharp fifth of the complete C altered dominant and avoids the less idiomatic Ab11#5 primary label. Tonal agrees with the C reading and lists F#9#11/Ab as the alternate; music21 prefers F#9/G# add B#. The remaining disagreement is a valid tritone-sub ambiguity.

0-1-4-6-8-10_b10.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-6-8-10_b10.note: C7#5(b9,#11)/Bb is preferred because Bb is the dominant seventh of the complete C altered dominant. Tonal agrees and lists the valid F#9#11/Bb alternate; music21's Gm9/Bb add C is semantically the same F#9#11 pitch-class structure. This is a genuine altered-dominant vs tritone-related split, not a reason to re-rank away from C.

0-1-4-5-8_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-5-8_b0.note: Fm(maj7)♭13/C is preferred because the voicing cleanly contains F-A♭-C-E as a complete F minor-major seventh with D♭ as flat-thirteen color and C as the fifth in the bass. Tonal agrees as FmMaj7b6/C. Music21's D♭m(maj7)/C add F respells the same pitch set with both minor and major thirds, which is less useful as the primary musician-facing label.

0-1-4-5-8_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-5-8_b4.note: Fm(maj7)♭13/E is preferred because E is the major seventh of the complete F-A♭-C-E shell and D♭ is conventional flat-thirteen color. Tonal agrees. Music21's C♯m(maj7)/E add E♯ treats F as an added major third above C♯ while also keeping E as the minor third, so the split reflects oracle spelling limits rather than a stronger C♯-rooted analysis.

0-1-4-5-8_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-5-8_b8.note: Fm(maj7)♭13/A♭ is preferred because A♭ is the minor third of a complete F minor-major seventh sonority with D♭ as flat thirteenth. Tonal agrees. Music21's C♯m(maj7)/G♯ add E♯ preserves related pitch classes but requires simultaneous minor and major thirds, making it less idiomatic than the direct Fm(maj7)♭13 reading.

0-1-4-7_b1.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-7_b1.note: D♭m(maj7)#11 is correct; near-tie cluster resolved by root-position preference and fully-explained-voicing tie-breakers; both oracles produce related Db-rooted or diminished readings; sound

0-1-4-7_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-7_b0.note: Cadd♭9 is preferred because C-E-G is a complete root-position C major triad and D♭ is the single chromatic flat-nine color. This is the musician-facing name for the observed voicing, not plain C major. Music21's surface label CaddC# is semantically equivalent, while tonal's CMaddb9 normalization reports a major-add-flat-nine vocabulary mismatch rather than a stronger alternate root.

0-1-4-7_b4.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-7_b4.note: Cadd♭9/E is preferred because E is the third of a complete C major triad and D♭ remains the only added chromatic color. Music21's C/E add C# is semantically the same voicing, and tonal's CMaddb9/E agrees on the C-rooted add-flat-nine structure despite normalization differences. The E-rooted alternatives are less idiomatic because they recast the complete C triad as altered minor or diminished material.

0-1-4-7-10_b1.label: clearly-correct
0-1-4-7-10_b1.note: A♯dim7(add9) / C♯ is correct; music21 agrees; tonal drops the 9; pychord has no label

0-1-4-7-10_b10.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-4-7-10_b10.note: A♯dim7(add9) is correct; symmetric dim7 near-tie cluster with Gdim7(add11)/Bb and Edim7(b13)/Bb; root-position tie-breaker resolves correctly; C7b9/Bb is a legitimate fourth choice placed appropriately lower

0-1-4-7-8_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-4-7-8_b0.note: A♭maj7♯5(add11) / C is defensible (conventional 3rd-in-bass; ♯5 absorbs E); music21's D♭m(maj7)♯11 isn't authoritative; tonal and pychord decline (non-idiomatic voicing)

0-1-4-8_b0.label: clearly-correct
0-1-4-8_b0.note: C♯m(maj7)/B♯ is correct; clean structural win over Caugadd♭9 (all 4 notes as core template tones vs 1 extra); tonal agrees; music21's A♭+(add11)/C corresponds to our 4th choice; sound

0-1-4-7_b7.label: clearly-correct
0-1-4-7_b7.note: Cadd♭9/G is the clearest interpretation because it preserves a complete C-major triad in conventional second inversion and names D♭ as the single chromatic flat ninth. Music21 and tonal both agree on the C-rooted add-flat-nine structure. The E-rooted alternatives require less conventional mixed color tones, while C♯dim/G drops the sounding C.

0-1-5-7-9_b7.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-7-9_b7.note: A7♯5♯9/G is preferred because A-C♯-E♯-G is a complete altered dominant seventh in third inversion, with C/B♯ as conventional sharp-nine color. Tonal agrees. Music21's F/G add C♯ keeps an F major shell but treats G as an added ninth and C♯ as chromatic color, so it misses the chord-defining dominant seventh.

0-1-5-7-9_b9.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-7-9_b9.note: A7♯5♯9 is preferred because A is the bass/root and the voicing names every tone as root, major third, sharp fifth, flat seventh, and sharp ninth of a standard altered dominant. Tonal agrees, and ChoCo common-name counts strongly favor dominant7-sharp5 sharp-nine signatures over sparse major add-tone variants. Music21's F/A add G,C♯ is an upper-structure major-triad label that drops the primary dominant identity.

0-1-5-9_b1.label: clearly-correct
0-1-5-9_b1.note: D♭maj7♯5 is correct; clean structural win over C♯maj7♭13 (baked-in #5 vs b13 alteration); tonal lists both; music21's A+/C# addB# corresponds to our distant 5th choice; not actionable

0-1-5-6-10_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b0.note: G♭maj7♯11/C is preferred because the voicing contains the complete G♭ major-seventh shell G♭-B♭-D♭-F with C as ♯11. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F♯maj♯4/C keeps the Lydian color but drops the major seventh from its normalized structure, so the split reflects oracle vocabulary rather than an engine issue.

0-1-5-6-10_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b1.note: G♭maj7♯11/D♭ is preferred because D♭ is the fifth of a complete G♭maj7♯11 voicing. Music21 agrees semantically as F♯maj7/C♯ add B♯. Tonal's F♯maj♯4/D♭ omits the major seventh in normalization, making it a less complete primary label.

0-1-5-6-10_b5.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b5.note: G♭maj7♯11/F is preferred because F is the major seventh in the bass and the voicing contains root, third, fifth, major seventh, and ♯11. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F♯maj♯4/F captures the root and Lydian color but does not preserve the full maj7♯11 identity.

0-1-5-6-10_b6.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b6.note: G♭maj7♯11 is preferred because it is the complete root-position Lydian major-seventh sonority. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F♯maj♯4 is an enharmonic Lydian-major label, but its normalized structure omits the major seventh; ChoCo also contains maj7♯11 observations, so retaining the seventh is musically justified.

0-1-5-6-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-5-6-10_b10.note: G♭maj7♯11/B♭ is preferred because B♭ is the third of a complete G♭maj7♯11 voicing. Music21 agrees semantically. Tonal's F♯maj♯4/B♭ gives the Lydian color but drops the major seventh from the comparable structure, so it is not a better musician-facing answer.

0-1-5-7-10_b5.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-5-7-10_b5.note: B♭m6/9/F is preferred because it is a complete minor-six-nine chord with its fifth in the bass, a more conventional inversion than the complete Gm7♭5(add11)/F reading with its flat seventh in the bass. Music21 chooses the valid G-rooted half-diminished interpretation, while tonal includes B♭m6/9/F. Related root-bass cases favor their respective structures, and key context does not resolve this F-bass ambiguity.

0-1-6-8-10_b1.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-6-8-10_b1.note: B♭m9♯5/D♭ is preferred because the voicing contains the complete B♭ minor-seventh-sharp-five shell B♭-D♭-F♯-A♭ with C as the ninth, and D♭ is the minor third in the bass. Tonal agrees. Music21's F♯/C♯ add G♯,B♯ captures an upper-structure major shell but drops the defining minor seventh and sharp-fifth quality.

0-1-6-8-10_b8.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-6-8-10_b8.note: B♭m9♯5/A♭ is preferred because A♭ is the flat seventh of a complete B♭m9♯5 sonority. Tonal agrees. Music21's F♯/G♯ add B♯ is a partial major/add-tone reading of the same pitch classes, but it omits the B♭-D♭-F♯-A♭ minor-seventh-sharp-five structure.

0-1-6-8-10_b10.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-6-8-10_b10.note: B♭m9♯5 is preferred because it is root position and names every tone as part of a complete minor-seventh-sharp-five ninth chord. Tonal agrees. Music21's G♭/B♭ add A♭,C is a related major-shell bookkeeping label, but it does not preserve the complete B♭ minor seventh quality.

0-1-6-9_b6.label: clearly-correct
0-1-6-9_b6.note: F♯m♯11 is the clearest isolated-voicing name because F♯ is the bass and root of a complete minor triad, with C/B♯ supplying a single sharp-eleventh color. Music21's F♯dimaddC♯ requires contradictory flat-five and perfect-fifth roles, while tonal's D♭maj7♯5sus4/F♯ is a highly altered suspended slash interpretation. Key context does not displace the structurally direct F♯-rooted reading.

0-1-6-9_b9.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-1-6-9_b9.note: A6add#9 is correct; the A bass supports a root-position split-third sixth chord with an optional omitted fifth, while F#m#11/A is a strong alternate that preserves a complete minor triad but spells C as B#; music21 and tonal choose less conventional variants of those competing structures; key context does not resolve the chromatic-color ambiguity; no engine change needed

0-2-4-8_b2.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-2-4-8_b2.note: E7#5/D is correct; the notes are the augmented triad C-E-G# plus D, and E is the only augmented-equivalent root whose b7 (D) is present, making E7#5 the unique complete four-note reading with the bass as a chord tone (third inversion). Caug(add9)/D (music21, tonal #1) is the salient triad-first reading but strands the bass D as the added 9th. tonal corroborates E7#5/D at #2. Mirrors the b0 sibling, where C-in-bass correctly prefers root-position Caugadd9: an aug-member bass favors the augmented reading, a non-member bass favors the complete dominant whose bass is a core tone. Sound; no engine change needed.

0-2-4-6-8-10_b0.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-6-8-10_b0.note: C9#5#11 is preferred because this is the complete whole-tone collection and C is the bass/root, so C-D-E-F#-G#/Ab-Bb reads directly as a root-position dominant 9#5#11. Tonal agrees and lists the other whole-tone re-roots as alternates. Music21's Bb9#5/C add E keeps the same symmetric material but chooses a non-bass root and demotes the #11 to an added tone.

0-2-4-6-8-10_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-6-8-10_b2.note: D9#5#11 is preferred because the whole-tone collection is symmetric and D is the bass/root, yielding a complete root-position dominant 9#5#11. Tonal agrees and lists the other whole-tone re-roots as alternates. Music21's C9#5/D add F# chooses a different symmetric root and treats the bass-rooted #11 as an add tone.

0-2-4-6-8-10_b6.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-6-8-10_b6.note: Gb9#5#11 is preferred because Gb/F# is the bass/root of a complete whole-tone dominant 9#5#11 spelling. Tonal agrees semantically as F#9#5#11 and lists the other symmetric re-roots as alternates. Music21's E9#5/F# add A# is a related whole-tone re-rooting but less direct for a context-free bass-rooted chord label.

0-2-4-9_b0.label: context-dependent
0-2-4-9_b0.note: C6/9 is correct in C major; matches music21's Am/CaddD in A:min where Amadd11/C is #1; tonal-context-dependent ambiguity, not a disagreement

0-2-4-9_b2.label: oracle-limitation
0-2-4-9_b2.note: D7sus2 is a complete root-position reading that explains every tone without alterations; D9(no3) is a more traditional name for the same voicing, but omission notation is outside the current vocabulary; music21's Am/D is a valid upper-structure description, while tonal's E-root altered sus reading is less direct; ChoCo has no dominant7sus2 observations

0-2-5-8_b8.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-2-5-8_b8.note: Fm6/Ab is correct; genuine Fm6↔Dø7/Ab ambiguity with all oracles listing both; tie-breaker prefers more conventional inversion (m3 in bass over b5 in bass)

0-2-5-7_b0.label: clearly-correct
0-2-5-7_b0.note: Csus2sus4 is correct; it is the complete root-position double-suspension template and scores clearly above the incomplete or slash alternatives. Tonal's Csus24 and pychord's Csus4add2 are semantically equivalent names for the same C-rooted structure.

0-2-5-7_b2.label: genuine-ambiguity
0-2-5-7_b2.note: Dm7(add11) is preferred with D in the bass because D, F, C, and G supply the root, minor third, flat seventh, and eleventh of a conventional root-position minor-eleventh shell with an omitted fifth. Tonal's D-rooted first choice supports only the root hearing, not the full minor-eleventh label. G7sus4/D and Csus2sus4/D are complete and valid slash-chord alternatives, so the ambiguity is genuine, but their roots do not align with the bass.

0-1-3-6-8-10_b6.label: oracle-limitation
0-1-3-6-8-10_b6.note: G♭6/9♯11 is correct; the notes are degrees 1-6 of G♭ Lydian over a root-position G♭ major triad, the canonical major six-nine ♯11 sound. Music21's analogous A♭11/G♭ stacks thirds off the ♭7 into a complete dominant eleventh whose major-third-against-eleventh clash (C/D♭) is the dissonance that name avoids; tonal unavailable, pychord no label. We prefer the root-position Lydian reading; the ♯11 now scores as natural major-family color rather than an alteration.
